title.en: TensorBench: Benchmarking Coding Agents on a Compiler-Based Tensor Framework
title.zh: TensorBench：在基于编译器的张量框架上对编码智能体进行基准测试

abstract: Repository-level coding benchmarks face a trade-off between task difficulty and evaluation reliability: tasks that challenge frontier models often involve large codebases with incomplete test coverage, while human review does not scale. We introduce TensorBench, a benchmark of 199 feature-addition and refactoring tasks on an open-source compiler-based tensor framework that extends PyTorch with first-class support for dense and sparse tensors. Tasks cover new sparse formats, dense optimization passes, IR transformations, scheduler changes, runtime components, and high-level numerical operators. TensorBench grades each run by applying the agent's patch and running the framework's test suite, which includes the pre-existing randomized regression tests and any tests the agent adds. For feature-addition tasks, a pass means that the patched repository preserves the tested pre-existing behavior and satisfies the agent-added checks for the requested feature. We evaluate seven coding agents spanning three frontier model families and one open-weight model. Pass rates under this criterion range from $64.8\%$ for the strongest agent to $22.1\%$ for the weakest. Agents pass different subsets of tasks: pairwise Cohen's $κ$ ranges from $-0.07$ to $0.43$, with $κ= 0.05$ for the two strongest agents.

relevance.en: TensorBench provides 199 real-world feature-addition/refactoring tasks on a PyTorch tensor compiler, graded by full test suite including regression tests—directly addressing your need for rigorous repo-level evaluation beyond pass@k. It does not study post-training itself, but offers a challenging testbed where such techniques could be assessed.
relevance.zh: TensorBench 在 PyTorch 张量编译器上提供了 199 个真实世界的特征添加/重构任务，通过包括回归测试在内的完整测试套件进行评分——直接满足了您对超越 pass@k 的严格仓库级评估的需求。它本身并未研究后训练，但提供了一个具有挑战性的测试平台，可以在此类技术上进行评估。

figures.label: Figure 1

caption.en:
Example TensorBench task from the API category: feature_outer_product. Top: the
agent’s prompt. Bottom-left: excerpt of code produced by Claude 4.7. Bottom-right: one of the
agent-added tests.
caption.zh: 来自 API 类别的 TensorBench 任务示例：feature_outer_product。上方：智能体的提示。左下方：Claude 4.7 生成的代码片段。右下方：智能体添加的其中一个测试。

figures.reference_text: Descriptions state the requested behavior and the tests the agent should add. To make benchmark construction scalable, tasks do not provide test inputs; the agent is expected to write its own tests. Grading uses both the agent’s tests and the pre-existing suite. Figure 1 traces one task end-to-end. The feature_outer_product task, drawn from the API category, asks for a sparse outer-product API. The agent’s patch illustrates a typical API-task implementation path: it constructs a CIN expression, lowers it through CINLowerer and LLIRLowerer, and JIT-compiles the result via load_inline. Many API tasks share this structure: a small user-visible addition requires changes to the same three-level IR that pure-IR and codegen tasks modify directly.

figures.label: Figure 2

caption.en:
Evaluation pipeline. The agent operates inside a containerized clone of the repository at the
task’s base commit. To grade the run, the harness applies the extracted patch in a fresh container,
rebuilds the C++ runtime, and executes the full test suite.
caption.zh: 评估流程。智能体在任务基础提交处仓库的容器化副本内操作。为了对运行进行评分，测试工具在一个新容器中应用提取的补丁，重建 C++ 运行时，并执行完整的测试套件。

figures.reference_text: For each task, the harness (Figure 2) builds a Docker image at the task’s base_commit, starts the agent in a frozen working copy, and extracts the resulting code changes as a unified diff against that commit. It then applies the diff in a fresh container, rebuilds the C++ runtime, and runs the test suite. The task is marked successful iff every test in the post-patch suite passes. Section B gives the full pipeline, including image construction and output parsing; Section K describes the released harness, dataset, Dockerfile, and grading strategy.

caption.en:
Evaluation pipeline. The agent operates inside a containerized clone of the repository at the
task’s base commit. To grade the run, the harness applies the extracted patch in a fresh container,
rebuilds the C++ runtime, and executes the full test suite.
caption.zh: 评估流程。智能体在任务基础提交处仓库的容器化副本内操作。为了对运行进行评分，测试工具在一个新容器中应用提取的补丁，重建 C++ 运行时，并执行完整的测试套件。

figure.reference_text: For each task, the harness (Figure 2) builds a Docker image at the task’s base_commit, starts the agent in a frozen working copy, and extracts the resulting code changes as a unified diff against that commit. It then applies the diff in a fresh container, rebuilds the C++ runtime, and runs the test suite. The task is marked successful iff every test in the post-patch suite passes. Section B gives the full pipeline, including image construction and output parsing; Section K describes the released harness, dataset, Dockerfile, and grading strategy.

brief_summary.en:
Existing repository-level coding benchmarks face a fundamental trade-off: tasks that challenge frontier models often involve large codebases with incomplete test coverage, making automated evaluation unreliable, while human review does not scale. TensorBench addresses this by introducing a benchmark of 199 feature-addition and refactoring tasks on an open-source compiler-based tensor framework (Finch) that extends PyTorch. Unlike prior benchmarks, TensorBench provides a rigorous automated evaluation that requires a patch to both preserve all pre-existing randomized regression tests and satisfy new tests an agent writes for the requested feature.

The evaluation pipeline applies each agent's patch in a containerized environment, rebuilds the C++ runtime, then runs the full test suite. On this benchmark, seven coding agents achieved pass rates from 64.8% (Claude 4.7) down to 22.1% (Claude 4.5), with the two strongest agents showing very low agreement (Cohen's κ = 0.05) on which tasks they solve. This orthogonal success pattern suggests that no single model is likely to master the full diversity of compiler-based framework tasks, and that ensemble or multi-agent strategies could outperform any individual system by exploiting their complementary strengths.
brief_summary.zh:
现有的仓库级编码基准面临一个根本性的权衡：挑战前沿模型的任务通常涉及代码库庞大且测试覆盖不全的问题，这使得自动化评估不可靠，而人工审查又难以规模化。TensorBench 通过引入一个包含 199 个特征添加和重构任务的基准测试来解决这个问题，该基准测试基于一个开源的、基于编译器的张量框架 (Finch)，该框架扩展了 PyTorch。与之前的基准不同，TensorBench 提供了严格的自动化评估，要求补丁既要保留所有预先存在的随机化回归测试，又要满足智能体为所请求特征编写的新测试。

评估流程在一个容器化环境中应用每个智能体的补丁，重建 C++ 运行时，然后运行完整的测试套件。在此基准测试上，七个编码智能体的通过率从 64.8%（Claude 4.7）到 22.1%（Claude 4.5）不等，其中两个最强的智能体在它们能解决的任务上表现出极低的一致性（Cohen's κ = 0.05）。这种正交的成功模式表明，没有单一模型能够掌握基于编译器的框架任务的全部多样性，而集成或多智能体策略可以利用它们互补的优势超越任何单个系统。

problem.en: Existing repository-level coding benchmarks face a fundamental trade-off between task difficulty and evaluation reliability. Tasks that challenge frontier models often involve large codebases with incomplete test coverage, making automated evaluation unreliable, while human review does not scale. There is a lack of benchmarks that combine realistic, complex coding tasks (feature addition and refactoring) on a real, non-trivial codebase with a comprehensive, automated evaluation through an extensive test suite that includes randomized regression tests. Prior benchmarks either use synthetic or small-scale tasks, or lack thorough testing, leading to evaluation noise and inability to detect regressions. TensorBench fills this gap by providing 199 tasks on the Finch tensor compiler (extending PyTorch with first-class support for dense and sparse tensors), covering diverse areas like new sparse formats, dense optimization passes, IR transformations, scheduler changes, runtime components, and high-level numerical operators, with a rigorous grading mechanism that checks both pre-existing behavior preservation and new feature correctness.
problem.zh: 现有的仓库级编码基准面临任务难度与评估可靠性之间的根本权衡。挑战前沿模型的任务通常涉及代码库庞大且测试覆盖不全的问题，使得自动化评估不可靠，而人工审查又难以规模化。缺乏一个能够将现实、复杂的编码任务（特征添加和重构）与一个真实、非平凡的代码库以及通过包含随机化回归测试的广泛测试套件进行的全面自动化评估相结合的基准。先前的基准要么使用合成或小规模任务，要么缺乏彻底的测试，导致评估噪音且无法检测回归。TensorBench 通过在 Finch 张量编译器（扩展 PyTorch，为密集和稀疏张量提供一等支持）上提供 199 个任务来填补这一空白，这些任务涵盖新稀疏格式、密集优化传递、IR 转换、调度器更改、运行时组件和高级数值算子等不同领域，并采用严格的评分机制，同时检查预先存在行为的保留和新特征的正确性。

solution.en: The main contribution is TensorBench, a benchmark and evaluation framework for coding agents that provides a reliable, scalable, and challenging testbed. The key insight is to use an open-source compiler-based tensor framework (Finch) that has a rich, existing test suite with randomized regression tests, and to design tasks that require agents to add new features or refactor components while preserving all existing behavior. Unlike prior benchmarks that rely on human judgment or limited test coverage, TensorBench automates evaluation by applying the agent's patch, rebuilding the C++ runtime, and running the full test suite, including both the pre-existing tests and any tests the agent adds. This ensures that a pass indicates both no regression and correct implementation of the requested feature. The benchmark covers a wide range of realistic software engineering tasks, from new sparse formats to scheduler changes, and evaluates agents on a real-world codebase, providing a more accurate assessment of their ability to handle complex repository-level changes.
solution.zh: 主要贡献是 TensorBench，一个用于编码智能体的基准和评估框架，它提供了一个可靠、可扩展且具有挑战性的测试平台。关键洞察在于使用一个开源的、基于编译器的张量框架 (Finch)，该框架拥有丰富的、包含随机化回归测试的现有测试套件，并设计需要智能体在保留所有现有行为的同时添加新特征或重构组件的任务。与依赖人工判断或有限测试覆盖率的先前基准不同，TensorBench 通过应用智能体的补丁、重建 C++ 运行时并运行完整的测试套件（包括预先存在的测试和智能体添加的任何测试）来自动化评估。这确保了通过既表示没有回归，也表示正确实现了所请求的特征。该基准涵盖了一系列真实的软件工程任务，从新的稀疏格式到调度器更改，并在真实世界的代码库上评估智能体，从而更准确地评估它们处理复杂仓库级更改的能力。

methodology.en: TensorBench consists of 199 tasks (124 feature addition, 75 refactoring) on the Finch tensor compiler repository, which extends PyTorch with first-class support for dense and sparse tensors via a tensor compiler. Tasks span six categories: new sparse formats, dense optimization passes, IR transformations, scheduler changes, runtime components, and high-level numerical operators (API). Each task is defined by a description (374-3,789 characters) specifying the desired behavior, but not providing test inputs; agents must write their own tests. The evaluation pipeline (Figure 2) is containerized: for each task, a Docker image is built at the task's base commit, the agent operates on a frozen working copy, and its code changes are extracted as a unified diff. In a fresh container, the diff is applied, the C++ runtime is rebuilt, and the full test suite is executed. A task is marked successful iff every test passes — all pre-existing tests (including randomized regression tests) and any tests added by the agent. For feature-addition tasks, this means preserving tested pre-existing behavior and satisfying the agent-added checks. Agents are given a limited number of attempts (budget) per task; they can iterate and run tests themselves during development, but the final grading is done externally. The benchmark uses commit-level base points to ensure tasks are up-to-date. Seven agents are evaluated: Claude 4.7, 4.6, 4.5; Codex 5.5, 5.4, 5.3; Gemini 3.1; and Qwen3. Agents are compared via pass rates and pairwise Cohen's kappa for task overlap.
methodology.zh: TensorBench 包含 199 个任务（124 个特征添加，75 个重构），这些任务基于 Finch 张量编译器仓库，该仓库通过张量编译器为 PyTorch 扩展了密集和稀疏张量的一等支持。任务涵盖六个类别：新稀疏格式、密集优化传递、IR 转换、调度器更改、运行时组件和高级数值算子（API）。每个任务由一个描述（374-3,789 个字符）定义，该描述指定了所需的行为，但不提供测试输入；智能体必须编写自己的测试。评估流程（图 2）是容器化的：对于每个任务，在任务的基础提交处构建一个 Docker 镜像，智能体在一个冻结的工作副本上操作，其代码更改被提取为统一差异。在一个新的容器中，应用差异，重建 C++ 运行时，并执行完整的测试套件。当且仅当每个测试都通过时，任务才被标记为成功——所有预先存在的测试（包括随机化回归测试）以及智能体添加的任何测试。对于特征添加任务，这意味着保留经过测试的预先存在的行为并满足智能体添加的检查。智能体在每个任务上获得有限次数的尝试（预算）；他们可以在开发过程中迭代并自行运行测试，但最终评分是外部进行的。基准测试使用提交级基础点来确保任务是最新的。评估了七个智能体：Claude 4.7、4.6、4.5；Codex 5.5、5.4、5.3；Gemini 3.1；和 Qwen3。智能体通过通过率和成对 Cohen's kappa 在任务重叠方面进行比较。

experiments.en: The experiments evaluate seven coding agents on TensorBench's 199 tasks. Pass rates range from 64.8% (Claude 4.7) to 22.1% (Claude 4.5). The strongest agent, Claude 4.7, achieved 64.8% pass rate, followed by Codex 5.5 at 55.3%, Codex 5.4 at 54.8%, Gemini 3.1 at 48.2%, Codex 5.3 at 42.2%, Qwen3 at 32.7%, Claude 4.6 at 29.1%, and Claude 4.5 at 22.1%. Pairwise Cohen's kappa ranges from -0.07 to 0.43, indicating low agreement on which tasks are solved; notably, κ=0.05 between the two strongest agents (Claude 4.7 and Codex 5.5), meaning they pass very different subsets. Combined, they cover 168/199 tasks (84.4%). Failure mode analysis (Figure 4) shows that the most common failure type is 'partial new tests pass' for some agents, and regression failures (breaking pre-existing tests) are also common (44-65% of failures). Patch size analysis (Figure 6) reveals that failed patches tend to be larger than successful ones for most agents (e.g., Codex 5.3: 485 vs. 758 median lines). Pass rate by description length (Figure 7) shows that most agents decline in the top quartile of description length, but Claude 4.7 improves on the longest descriptions.
experiments.zh: 实验在 199 个 TensorBench 任务上评估了七个编码智能体。通过率从 64.8%（Claude 4.7）到 22.1%（Claude 4.5）不等。最强智能体 Claude 4.7 实现了 64.8% 的通过率，其次是 Codex 5.5 的 55.3%、Codex 5.4 的 54.8%、Gemini 3.1 的 48.2%、Codex 5.3 的 42.2%、Qwen3 的 32.7%、Claude 4.6 的 29.1% 和 Claude 4.5 的 22.1%。成对 Cohen's kappa 范围为 -0.07 到 0.43，表明在哪些任务被解决上的一致性较低；值得注意的是，两个最强智能体（Claude 4.7 和 Codex 5.5）之间的 κ=0.05，意味着它们通过的子集非常不同。两者结合覆盖了 168/199 个任务（84.4%）。失败模式分析（图 4）显示，对于某些智能体，最常见的失败类型是“部分新测试通过”，并且回归失败（破坏预先存在的测试）也很常见（占失败的 44-65%）。补丁大小分析（图 6）显示，对于大多数智能体，失败的补丁往往比成功的补丁更大（例如，Codex 5.3：中位行数 485 vs. 758）。按描述长度的通过率（图 7）显示，大多数智能体在描述长度的上四分位数中表现下降，但 Claude 4.7 在最长的描述上有所改进。

conclusion.en: The benchmark reveals that even the best agent, Claude 4.7, achieves only a 64.8% pass rate, confirming substantial room for progress in repository-level code synthesis. Crucially, the very low agreement between top agents (κ=0.05) indicates they succeed on largely disjoint tasks, which strongly suggests that combining complementary agents could outperform any single model. The evaluation design, which requires patches to both pass existing regression tests and satisfy agent-added tests, provides a rigorous multi-objective signal that exposes agents' ongoing difficulty with introducing regressions. For researchers, these findings imply that advancing beyond current limits may require ensemble or multi-agent strategies rather than improving a single model, and that compiler-based frameworks pose unique challenges due to tightly coupled internal layers where cross-file changes can cascade into runtime failures.
conclusion.zh: 该基准测试揭示，即使是最好的智能体 Claude 4.7，也只有 64.8% 的通过率，确认了仓库级代码合成仍有很大的进步空间。至关重要的是，顶级智能体之间极低的一致性（κ=0.05）表明它们在大部分不相交的任务上取得成功，这强烈表明组合互补的智能体可以胜过任何单一模型。评估设计要求补丁既要通过现有的回归测试，又要满足智能体添加的测试，这提供了一个严格的多目标信号，暴露了智能体在引入回归方面持续存在的困难。对于研究人员来说，这些发现意味着超越当前极限可能需要集成或多智能体策略，而不是改进单一模型，并且基于编译器的框架由于紧密耦合的内部层（跨文件更改可能导致运行时失败）而带来了独特的挑战。

question.en: TensorBench's evaluation framework enforces that a passing patch must both preserve pre-existing randomized regression tests and satisfy new, agent-added tests for the requested feature.  How does this dual-criterion design improve upon traditional pass@k or compilation-only metrics for measuring correctness in repository-level code synthesis, and what implications does this have for designing execution-feedback signals in post-training (e.g., RL with execution feedback)?
question.zh: TensorBench 的评估框架强制要求通过补丁必须同时保留预先存在的随机化回归测试，并满足智能体为所请求特征添加的新测试。这种双标准设计如何改进传统的 pass@k 或仅编译指标在测量仓库级代码合成正确性方面的不足，以及这对在后训练中设计执行反馈信号（例如，带有执行反馈的强化学习）有何启示？

reason.en: This question directly addresses the user's interest in evaluation methodologies that go beyond pass@k on isolated functions (must-have keyword: 'software engineering benchmarks'). It also probes the design of execution feedback, which is central to the user's research focus on post-training techniques like RL with execution feedback (must-have keyword: 'execution feedback').  The answer is directly supported by the paper's grading methodology and its critique of existing benchmarks.
reason.zh: 这个问题直接涉及用户对超越孤立函数 pass@k 的评估方法的兴趣（必选关键词：'软件工程基准'）。它还探讨了执行反馈的设计，这是用户对后训练技术（如带有执行反馈的 RL）研究关注的核心（必选关键词：'执行反馈'）。答案直接由论文的评分方法及其对现有基准的评述支持。

question.en: The paper reports a very low pairwise agreement (Cohen's κ = 0.05) between the two strongest agents (Claude 4.7 and Codex 5.5).  For a researcher interested in post-training for repo-level code synthesis, what does this orthogonal success pattern suggest about the potential of ensemble or complementarity-based strategies (e.g., multi-agent collaboration, or using one agent's outputs as training data for another) compared to trying to train a single monolithic model via SFT or RLHF?
question.zh: 论文报告了两个最强智能体（Claude 4.7 和 Codex 5.5）之间的成对一致性非常低（Cohen's κ = 0.05）。对于一位对仓库级代码合成的后训练感兴趣的研究人员来说，这种正交的成功模式对比于试图通过 SFT 或 RLHF 训练单一单体模型，对集成或互补性策略（例如，多智能体协作，或使用一个智能体的输出作为另一个智能体的训练数据）的潜力有何启示？

reason.en: This question ties the paper's key empirical finding (low inter-agent agreement) to the user's explicit interest in post-training techniques (SFT, RLHF/RLAIF) and how to improve correctness on real-world tasks (must-have keyword: 'post-training').  It moves beyond surface-level comparison to explore a strategic implication for agent design, which is a substantive thought-provoking direction for the user's research.
reason.zh: 这个问题将论文的关键实证发现（低智能体间一致性）与用户对后训练技术（SFT、RLHF/RLAIF）及如何提高真实世界任务正确性的明确兴趣联系起来（必选关键词：'后训练'）。它超越了表面比较，探索了对智能体设计的战略意义，这对用户的研究来说是一个实质性的、发人深省的方向。

question.en: TensorBench tasks span six categories (new sparse formats, dense optimization passes, IR transformations, scheduler changes, runtime components, numerical operators).  From a long-context handling perspective, which of these categories likely places the highest demands on the agent's ability to track cross-file dependencies and global invariants, and what evidence from the paper (e.g., per-category pass rates) supports or challenges this hypothesis?
question.zh: TensorBench 任务涵盖六个类别（新稀疏格式、密集优化传递、IR 转换、调度器更改、运行时组件、数值算子）。从长上下文处理的角度来看，这些类别中哪一个最可能对智能体追踪跨文件依赖关系和全局不变量的能力提出最高要求，论文中有哪些证据（例如，按类别的通过率）支持或挑战了这一假设？

reason.en: This question directly targets the user's specific interest in 'long-context handling' and 'repository-level code synthesis' (must-have keywords).  It asks the user to extract and reason about evidence from the paper's experimental results (per-category success rates) to assess which task types stress long-context abilities. This is a concrete, analyzable question grounded in the paper's design.
reason.zh: 这个问题直接针对用户对“长上下文处理”和“仓库级代码合成”的具体兴趣（必选关键词）。它要求用户从论文的实验结果（按类别成功率）中提取并推理证据，以评估哪些任务类型会考验长上下文能力。这是一个基于论文设计的、具体且可分析的问题。

question.en: The benchmark uses an open-source compiler-based tensor framework (Finch) that extends PyTorch.  For the user's interest in repository-level integration, what unique challenges does a compiler-based codebase (with IR passes, schedulers, and runtime components) pose for LLM-based code synthesis compared to typical application-level repositories, and how do the TensorBench tasks specifically stress integration with existing codebase conventions and test infrastructure?
question.zh: 该基准使用了一个开源的、基于编译器的张量框架 (Finch)，该框架扩展了 PyTorch。对于用户关注的仓库级集成，与典型的应用程序级仓库相比，基于编译器的代码库（具有 IR 传递、调度器和运行时组件）为基于 LLM 的代码合成带来了哪些独特挑战，TensorBench 任务如何特别强调与现有代码库约定和测试基础设施的集成？

reason.en: This question connects the paper's domain (compiler-based tensor framework) to the user's focus on 'repository-level code synthesis' and 'integration with existing codebases' (must-have keywords).  It encourages the user to think about how the nature of the codebase (compiler vs. application) influences the difficulty of integration, which is a nuanced consideration for evaluation design.  The paper's description of Finch and the task categories provides the material to answer comprehensively.
reason.zh: 这个问题将论文的领域（基于编译器的张量框架）与用户关注的“仓库级代码合成”和“与现有代码库集成”（必选关键词）联系起来。它鼓励用户思考代码库的性质（编译器 vs. 应用程序）如何影响集成的难度，这是评估设计中的一个细致考虑。论文对 Finch 和任务类别的描述为全面回答提供了材料。

question.en: TensorBench's evaluation framework enforces that a passing patch must both preserve pre-existing randomized regression tests and satisfy new, agent-added tests for the requested feature.  How does this dual-criterion design improve upon traditional pass@k or compilation-only metrics for measuring correctness in repository-level code synthesis, and what implications does this have for designing execution-feedback signals in post-training (e.g., RL with execution feedback)?
question.zh: TensorBench 的评估框架强制要求通过补丁必须同时保留预先存在的随机化回归测试，并满足智能体为所请求特征添加的新测试。这种双标准设计如何改进传统的 pass@k 或仅编译指标在测量仓库级代码合成正确性方面的不足，以及这对在后训练中设计执行反馈信号（例如，带有执行反馈的强化学习）有何启示？

reason.en: This question directly addresses the user's interest in evaluation methodologies that go beyond pass@k on isolated functions (must-have keyword: 'software engineering benchmarks'). It also probes the design of execution feedback, which is central to the user's research focus on post-training techniques like RL with execution feedback (must-have keyword: 'execution feedback').  The answer is directly supported by the paper's grading methodology and its critique of existing benchmarks.
reason.zh: 这个问题直接涉及用户对超越孤立函数 pass@k 的评估方法的兴趣（必选关键词：'软件工程基准'）。它还探讨了执行反馈的设计，这是用户对后训练技术（如带有执行反馈的 RL）研究关注的核心（必选关键词：'执行反馈'）。答案直接由论文的评分方法及其对现有基准的评述支持。

answer.en: TensorBench's dual-criterion design improves upon pass@k and compilation-only metrics by ensuring that a passing patch both "preserves the tested pre-existing behavior" and "satisfies the agent-added checks for the requested feature" (abstract). This directly addresses the trade-off between task difficulty and evaluation reliability, as compilation-only checks miss runtime errors and pass@k on isolated functions cannot verify repository-level integration. For execution-feedback signals in post-training (e.g., RL with execution feedback), this design provides a multi-objective reward: a patch receives positive feedback only if it does not break existing regression tests (safety) and fulfills new feature tests (correctness). This encourages agents to generate additional tests and maintain backward compatibility, offering a more nuanced signal than binary pass/fail on a single metric.
answer.zh: TensorBench 的双标准设计通过确保通过的补丁既“保留被测试的预先存在的行为”又“满足智能体为所请求特征添加的检查”（摘要），改进了 pass@k 和仅编译指标。这直接解决了任务难度和评估可靠性之间的权衡，因为仅编译检查会遗漏运行时错误，而孤立函数上的 pass@k 无法验证仓库级集成。对于后训练中的执行反馈信号（例如，带有执行反馈的 RL），这种设计提供了多目标奖励：只有当补丁不破坏现有的回归测试（安全性）并且满足新特征测试（正确性）时，它才会获得正面反馈。这鼓励智能体生成额外的测试并保持向后兼容性，提供了比单一指标上的二元通过/失败更细致的信号。

question.en: The paper reports a very low pairwise agreement (Cohen's κ = 0.05) between the two strongest agents (Claude 4.7 and Codex 5.5).  For a researcher interested in post-training for repo-level code synthesis, what does this orthogonal success pattern suggest about the potential of ensemble or complementarity-based strategies (e.g., multi-agent collaboration, or using one agent's outputs as training data for another) compared to trying to train a single monolithic model via SFT or RLHF?
question.zh: 论文报告了两个最强智能体（Claude 4.7 和 Codex 5.5）之间的成对一致性非常低（Cohen's κ = 0.05）。对于一位对仓库级代码合成的后训练感兴趣的研究人员来说，这种正交的成功模式对比于试图通过 SFT 或 RLHF 训练单一单体模型，对集成或互补性策略（例如，多智能体协作，或使用一个智能体的输出作为另一个智能体的训练数据）的潜力有何启示？

reason.en: This question ties the paper's key empirical finding (low inter-agent agreement) to the user's explicit interest in post-training techniques (SFT, RLHF/RLAIF) and how to improve correctness on real-world tasks (must-have keyword: 'post-training').  It moves beyond surface-level comparison to explore a strategic implication for agent design, which is a substantive thought-provoking direction for the user's research.
reason.zh: 这个问题将论文的关键实证发现（低智能体间一致性）与用户对后训练技术（SFT、RLHF/RLAIF）及如何提高真实世界任务正确性的明确兴趣联系起来（必选关键词：'后训练'）。它超越了表面比较，探索了对智能体设计的战略意义，这对用户的研究来说是一个实质性的、发人深省的方向。

answer.en: The paper does not directly discuss post-training strategies such as SFT, RLHF, or multi-agent ensemble approaches. However, the finding that "pairwise Cohen's κ ranges from −0.07 to 0.43, with κ = 0.05 for the two strongest agents" (Claude 4.7 and Codex 5.5) implies that these agents succeed on largely disjoint subsets of tasks. This orthogonal success pattern suggests that no single monolithic model, even under post-training, is likely to master the full diversity of compiler-based tensor framework tasks. In contrast, the near-zero agreement provides a strong empirical rationale for ensemble or complementarity-based strategies: combining agents that fail on different tasks could yield higher coverage than improving any one agent alone. The paper states that "agents pass different subsets of tasks," which directly supports the idea that a multi-agent collaboration or using one agent's outputs as training data for another could exploit this diversity to improve correctness on real-world tasks. Nonetheless, the paper itself does not implement or test any such post-training or ensemble methods.
answer.zh: 论文没有直接讨论诸如 SFT、RLHF 或多智能体集成方法等后训练策略。然而，“成对 Cohen's κ 范围为 -0.07 到 0.43，两个最强智能体（Claude 4.7 和 Codex 5.5）的 κ = 0.05”这一发现意味着这些智能体在大部分不相交的任务子集上取得成功。这种正交的成功模式表明，即使是经过后训练的单一单体模型，也不太可能掌握基于编译器的张量框架任务的全部多样性。相比之下，近乎为零的一致性为集成或互补性策略提供了强有力的实证依据：组合在不同任务上失败的智能体可以比单独改进任何一个智能体获得更高的覆盖率。论文指出“智能体通过不同的任务子集”，这直接支持了多智能体协作或使用一个智能体的输出作为另一个智能体的训练数据可以利用这种多样性来提高真实世界任务的正确性。尽管如此，论文本身并未实施或测试任何此类后训练或集成方法。

question.en: TensorBench tasks span six categories (new sparse formats, dense optimization passes, IR transformations, scheduler changes, runtime components, numerical operators).  From a long-context handling perspective, which of these categories likely places the highest demands on the agent's ability to track cross-file dependencies and global invariants, and what evidence from the paper (e.g., per-category pass rates) supports or challenges this hypothesis?
question.zh: TensorBench 任务涵盖六个类别（新稀疏格式、密集优化传递、IR 转换、调度器更改、运行时组件、数值算子）。从长上下文处理的角度来看，这些类别中哪一个最可能对智能体追踪跨文件依赖关系和全局不变量的能力提出最高要求，论文中有哪些证据（例如，按类别的通过率）支持或挑战了这一假设？

reason.en: This question directly targets the user's specific interest in 'long-context handling' and 'repository-level code synthesis' (must-have keywords).  It asks the user to extract and reason about evidence from the paper's experimental results (per-category success rates) to assess which task types stress long-context abilities. This is a concrete, analyzable question grounded in the paper's design.
reason.zh: 这个问题直接针对用户对“长上下文处理”和“仓库级代码合成”的具体兴趣（必选关键词）。它要求用户从论文的实验结果（按类别成功率）中提取并推理证据，以评估哪些任务类型会考验长上下文能力。这是一个基于论文设计的、具体且可分析的问题。

answer.en: The paper does not report per-category pass rates or directly compare the six task categories on any dimension, neither for long-context demands nor for success rates. The abstract lists the categories—“new sparse formats, dense optimization passes, IR transformations, scheduler changes, runtime components, and high-level numerical operators”—and states that agents “pass different subsets of tasks: pairwise Cohen's κ ranges from −0.07 to 0.43,” which shows task-level variation but is not broken down by category. Without category-level evidence such as pass rates, the paper provides no basis to determine which category places the highest demands on tracking cross-file dependencies and global invariants from a long-context handling perspective.
answer.zh: 论文没有报告按类别的通过率，也没有在任何维度上直接比较这六个任务类别，无论是在长上下文需求方面还是在成功率方面。摘要列出了这些类别——“新的稀疏格式、密集优化传递、IR 转换、调度器更改、运行时组件和高级数值算子”——并指出智能体“通过不同的任务子集：成对 Cohen's κ 范围为 -0.07 到 0.43”，这显示了任务级别的变化，但并未按类别细分。在没有类别级别证据（如通过率）的情况下，论文没有提供任何依据来确定哪个类别从长上下文处理的角度对追踪跨文件依赖关系和全局不变量提出了最高要求。

question.en: The benchmark uses an open-source compiler-based tensor framework (Finch) that extends PyTorch.  For the user's interest in repository-level integration, what unique challenges does a compiler-based codebase (with IR passes, schedulers, and runtime components) pose for LLM-based code synthesis compared to typical application-level repositories, and how do the TensorBench tasks specifically stress integration with existing codebase conventions and test infrastructure?
question.zh: 该基准使用了一个开源的、基于编译器的张量框架 (Finch)，该框架扩展了 PyTorch。对于用户关注的仓库级集成，与典型的应用程序级仓库相比，基于编译器的代码库（具有 IR 传递、调度器和运行时组件）为基于 LLM 的代码合成带来了哪些独特挑战，TensorBench 任务如何特别强调与现有代码库约定和测试基础设施的集成？

reason.en: This question connects the paper's domain (compiler-based tensor framework) to the user's focus on 'repository-level code synthesis' and 'integration with existing codebases' (must-have keywords).  It encourages the user to think about how the nature of the codebase (compiler vs. application) influences the difficulty of integration, which is a nuanced consideration for evaluation design.  The paper's description of Finch and the task categories provides the material to answer comprehensively.
reason.zh: 这个问题将论文的领域（基于编译器的张量框架）与用户关注的“仓库级代码合成”和“与现有代码库集成”（必选关键词）联系起来。它鼓励用户思考代码库的性质（编译器 vs. 应用程序）如何影响集成的难度，这是评估设计中的一个细致考虑。论文对 Finch 和任务类别的描述为全面回答提供了材料。

answer.en: The TensorBench paper does not explicitly articulate the unique challenges of a compiler-based codebase compared to typical application-level repositories. However, the nature of the tasks it defines—covering "new sparse formats, dense optimization passes, IR transformations, scheduler changes, runtime components, and high-level numerical operators"—implies that agents must manipulate multiple internal layers (IR passes, schedulers, runtime) that are tightly coupled, unlike isolated application logic. The requirement to "run the framework's test suite" which includes "pre-existing randomized regression tests" is the key stressor for integration: agents cannot simply add a feature in isolation; they must ensure their patch "preserves the tested pre-existing behavior" as measured by the full existing test infrastructure, and also satisfies "the agent-added checks for the requested feature." This demands that the synthesized code integrates without breaking any delicate cross-layer invariants enforced by the existing tests—a challenge amplified in a compiler codebase because changes in one pass or the scheduler can cascade into runtime correctness failures that a regression test suite would catch. The paper's evaluation criterion thus directly ties task success to compatibility with the codebase's conventions and test infrastructure, but it does not compare this difficulty to application-level repositories. Confidence: 0.4 (inferred from task categories and evaluation methodology, but the paper does not explicitly discuss the challenges of compiler-based vs. application-level codebases).
answer.zh: TensorBench 论文没有明确阐述基于编译器的代码库与典型的应用程序级仓库相比所面临的独特挑战。然而，它定义的任务性质——涵盖“新的稀疏格式、密集优化传递、IR 转换、调度器更改、运行时组件和高级数值算子”——意味着智能体必须操作多个紧密耦合的内部层（IR 传递、调度器、运行时），这与孤立的应用程序逻辑不同。要求“运行框架的测试套件”，其中包括“预先存在的随机化回归测试”，是集成的关键压力点：智能体不能孤立地添加特征；他们必须确保其补丁“保留被测试的预先存在的行为”，这是通过完整的现有测试基础设施来衡量的，并且还要满足“智能体为所请求特征添加的检查”。这要求合成的代码在不破坏现有测试所强制执行的任何微妙跨层不变量的情况下进行集成——这在编译器代码库中是一个被放大的挑战，因为一个传递或调度器的更改可能会级联导致回归测试套件能够捕获的运行时正确性失败。因此，论文的评估标准直接将任务成功与代码库约定和测试基础设施的兼容性联系起来，但它没有将这种困难与应用程序级仓库进行比较。置信度：0.4（从任务类别和评估方法推断，但论文没有明确讨论编译器代码库与应用程序级代码库相比的挑战）。
